one_line: Founder-figure of the Kashi surgical school, codifier of classical Indian operative medicine in the Gupta era.

appearance.age_look: Mid-30s, lean and wiry, with the calm bearing of a practiced healer.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones and a straight, medium-length nose; warm medium-brown complexion; sharp, focused dark-brown eyes with slightly thick eyebrows; a neatly kept short beard and moustache; default expression is serene yet intensely observant, as if always diagnosing.
appearance.hair: Black hair tied back into a tight topknot (small shikha) at the crown, with the rest cut short around the sides; a simple narrow white cloth band (like a plain headwrap strip) tied around the forehead to keep hair from the face while operating, knotted at the back with short ends.
appearance.costume: A knee-length off-white cotton angarkha-style wrap tunic, overlapping left over right and tied with hidden ties, with three-quarter sleeves that can be rolled just below the elbow; a light, unpatterned, undyed cotton dhoti in natural off-white wrapped in the north Indian style to mid-calf, neatly pleated at the front; a narrow, soft, pale-ochre cotton uttariya (shawl) draped from the left shoulder across the torso and around the back, leaving the right arm free for work; simple leather sandals with a toe loop; minimal jewellery—a single small copper bangle on the right wrist and a plain tulsi-bead-style necklace—to keep hands and forearms mostly bare for surgery; a small woven palm-leaf belt pouch at his right hip for herbs and small tools.
appearance.signature: A slender, slightly curved iron surgical knife (kshuraka) with a narrow blade about the length of his hand, attached to a smooth dark wooden handle wrapped in thin cord for grip, carried in a simple brown leather sheath tucked horizontally at the back of his waist, plus a small folded palm-leaf manuscript held in his left hand to suggest his role as teacher and codifier.

ref_desc: Sushruta is a lean, mid-30s Kashi surgeon with medium-brown skin, an oval face, short beard, black hair in a small topknot with a white forehead band, wearing an off-white angarkha, off-white dhoti, pale-ochre shawl, leather sandals, copper bangle, carrying a curved iron surgical knife in a back-waist sheath and a palm-leaf manuscript, in off-white, pale ochre, dark brown and leather-brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Sushruta the Surgeon: a lean, mid-30s North Indian man from Gupta-era Kashi with medium-brown skin, an oval face, sharp dark-brown eyes, and a neatly trimmed short beard and moustache, his black hair pulled into a small topknot at the crown with the sides cut short and a narrow white cloth band tied around his forehead, knotted at the back. Show him wearing a knee-length off-white cotton angarkha-style wrap tunic overlapping left over right with three-quarter sleeves, over an off-white cotton dhoti wrapped in a north Indian style to mid-calf, neat front pleats clearly visible. A pale-ochre cotton uttariya (shawl) is draped from his left shoulder across his chest and around his back, leaving his right arm free, and his feet are in simple dark-brown leather sandals with a toe loop. His only jewellery is a single small copper bangle on his right wrist and a plain bead necklace, with a small woven palm-leaf belt pouch hanging at his right hip. His signature weapon-prop is a slender slightly curved iron surgical knife with a narrow blade and dark wooden handle wrapped for grip, placed in a simple brown leather sheath tucked horizontally at the back of his waist, and he holds a folded palm-leaf manuscript in his left hand to suggest his role as medical author. Use a restrained palette of off-white for garments, pale ochre for the shawl, dark brown and leather brown for sandals, pouch and knife handle, and black for hair, keeping the overall look dignified, practical, and historically grounded in a Gupta-era medical setting.
